title: pittosporum abyssinicum subsp. engleri
intro: Pittosporum abyssinicum subsp. engleri, also known as the Engler's Pittosporum or African Pittosporum, is a plant species in the Pittosporaceae family. It has been traditionally used in Ethiopian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating respiratory infections, and relieving pain, as well as exhibiting potential antimalarial and antiviral effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Pittosporum abyssinicum subsp. engleri is often prepared as a tea or infusion, or used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
